description: Portable production-quality gates for product, vibe design, and vibe coding workflows. The gates are role-based and can migrate across digital office variants.

inspired_by.name: OpenHands
inspired_by.lesson: Use an explicit runtime harness with action execution, observation, and feedback loops instead of one-shot coding prompts.

inspired_by.name: SWE-agent
inspired_by.lesson: Keep the Agent-computer interface narrow, observable, and tied to issue-to-patch workflows.

inspired_by.name: aider
inspired_by.lesson: Use repository maps, git-visible changes, and automated commits/diffs as core coding-agent feedback.

inspired_by.name: SWE-bench
inspired_by.lesson: Judge coding agents with reproducible tasks and deterministic tests, not with self-review alone.

entry_contract: target user and job-to-be-done are stated
business outcome or project goal is stated
target viewport and device assumptions are clear
existing design system or visual direction is known
minimum_gates: text overflow and spacing risks are checked
accessibility contrast and keyboard path are checked
visual style is not one-note or generic

entry_contract: files or modules likely to change are identified
risk and rollback plan are known

quality_metrics.pass_rate: percentage of tasks passing deterministic gates
quality_metrics.rework_rate: number of user or reviewer correction loops
quality_metrics.route_stability: percentage of route tests with unchanged expected Agent/workflow
quality_metrics.design_escape_rate: visual or usability defects found after implementation
quality_metrics.time_to_green: time from task start to all gates passing
